呢一刻我覺得政府驚緊
前幾日林鄭都夠膽出力譴責，雖然無用
但今日記者會都唔開，佢怯緊
爭取五大訴求

U.S. senators push for earlier vote on Hong Kong rights bill as violence in the city rises. Under the collaboration of the communist driven Pan-Democrats, Hongkongers fell into CCP's plot and spoiled President Trump's schedule on China. Trump plans to coerce China to sign the trade deal and weaken China's economy and strengthen Hong Kong's economic foothold first, before he comes back for Hong Kong democracy. At the end, it's prosperity and democracy for Hong Kong, benefiting also the international community here.
I am isolated by the red press in Hong Kong. I have advised Hongkongers repeatedly on this: to raise the American flag, trust Trump's administration and refuse to act on the provocation of CCP. Without a single ounce of political tact, Hongkongers still reacted to the call to the streets and pretended to fight with the police after the police allowed one killing case and one rape case to be released to the press, made up a scene of well-spread riot and ruined their own future. Sacrifice of lives, perhaps for nothing, or for more sacrifices to come.
The Senate works on Realpolitik while the Congress works with populism. That's why USA's bicameral democracy beat the USSR thirty years ago. But this time, may not. Hong Kong is no West Berlin. It's east Berlin. Be realistic my American friends, the fall of Saigon will probably come to Hong Kong, because the American committed the same mistake to choose the communist-backed representatives here. This time we will produce no Stallone for Hollywood, sorry.
With the premature passing of the Hong Kong rights bill, CCP with play political trading with Trump and bargain on trade concessions with America. And the Chinese curse of human rights diplomacy in the days of Clinton and Obama come back to Trump. By shifting the original schedule, Trump may end up saving an East Berlin in Hong Kong, make himself the largest joke in American diplomacy.
You can't find political stupidity of this scale anywhere else in the world.
